card_page.policy_text: Get a Library Card - Wayland Free Public Library Please ensure Javascript is enabled for purposes of website accessibility Skip to content ***ATTENTION: The Library will be CLOSED Monday, 5/25, for Memorial Day*** 5 Concord Rd Wayland, MA 01778 Open now 9:00 am - 9:00 pm View All (508) 358-2311 Email Catalog Website Search Account Menu Close About Our Library About Staff Directory Location & Hours Policies Local History Newsletter & Sign-up Board of Trustees Employment Support Us The Impact of Executive Orders The Friends of Wayland Public Library Ongoing Book Sale Book & Media Donations Donate to the Library Volunteering Building Updates Exterior Site Improvements Wastewater Management System Interior Repairs and Improvements - Childrens Room and Public Restrooms Elevator Modernization HVAC Maintenance (Details coming soon) Events Programs All Programs Children's Programs Teen Programs Adult Programs Lifelong Learning 3D Design Tutorials and Videos Gossels Programs Great Presenters Series Program Recordings Services Using the Library My Account Get a Library Card Minuteman Mobile Borrowing Services Our Technology 3D Printing Book a Meeting Room Exhibits and Displays Policy Home Delivery Services Request a Purchase Your Next Read Adult Summer Reading 2025 Catalog Bookmatch Book Groups Read Against Racism Beyond Books English Language Learning (ESOL) Tech Help Seed Library Movies Library of Things Online Resources BPL eCard & Resources Museum Discount Passes Kids & Teens For Kids Overview Summer Reading Children’s Library Cards 1000 Books Before Kindergarten Literacy PLAY! Kits Online Resources for Kids Children’s Library of Things Games & Learning Websites for Kids Kids Read Against Racism Staff Picks For Teens Overview Summer Reading Teen Volunteering For Writers Test Prep and College Guides Study Help Staff Picks WHS History Project What can we help you find? Catalog Website Search Get a Library Card How do I obtain a library card? Come in person to the library with a photo ID and proof of your current Massachusetts address. Accepted forms of photo ID include, but are not limited to: state driver’s license or ID, passport, military ID, and student ID Accepted forms of current address include, but are not limited to: state driver’s license or ID, passport, bill, and government sent mail If your document has both a photo ID and your current address, that is the only document needed to obtain a library card. Children’s cards are issued in the Children’s Department (Lower Level). Please go here for more information. Any age person may have a library card. Can’t make it to the Library right now? You can get started with a temporary e-card here . Come into the library with proof of address to convert it into a standard account with full permissions. Avoid Duplicate Accounts: Had a Minuteman Library Card before, but unsure if it’s still active? Give us a call and we can confirm for you before online registration. I got a message that my card is expired or expiring— What do I do? Call or come into the library so that we can confirm that the contact information on record for you is still accurate (Did you move? Did you get rid of your landline? Are you a teen whose parent’s email is still primary on your account?). It’s a quick process that you can do in person or by phone. We’ll mark your account as up-to-date and your same card (and card number) will continue to be active. There is no need to register for a new card when you get this message. Some patrons are able to renew their library card online via Minuteman’s e-renew form. I already have a library card, can I get it on my smartphone? YES! The Minuteman Mobile app is available at the Apple App Store or Google Play Store. The app puts your library card AND your library in your pocket! Click the icon below for instructions and FAQ! What are the benefits associated with my card? As a Massachusetts resident and a member of the Minuteman Library Network, your card provides access to materials at more than forty libraries. With your card number and a PIN, you can browse our catalog , access databases , reserve museum passes , request materials, keep a reading history, view your own acc ount , and more. Do I need my card to check out library materials? The most efficient and reliable way to check out materials is to have your card. If you do not have it with you, a picture ID is acceptable. Other MLN libraries may require a card. How do I get more information? Questions about loan periods, renewals, returns, requests, and other borrowing services can be found on our Borrowing Services page. Questions about overdue charges or lost items can be answered on our Fine Policy page. Here is more information about the online catalog and your account . Related Pages Minuteman Mobile App Tax Forms 3D Printing Get a Library Card Borrowing Services Home Delivery Services Technology Contact Us Meeting Rooms ©2026 Wayland Free Public Library, All Rights Reserved. 5 Concord Rd Wayland, MA 01778 (508) 358-2311 info.wyl@minlib.net Subscribe to our Newsletter Website Design by Library Siteworks | A Division of Stirling Brandworks

pass_page.policy_text:
To limit museum types being displayed, check the corresponding categories:
To select a museum, click “Details/Reserve”, then select which Pass to reserve.

Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children."} Boston Children's Museum 308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210617-426-6500http://www.bostonchildrensmuseum.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston Children's Museum Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
Categories: Children, Crafts, Family, Technology
Pass Type: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
Pass Benefits This pass is an e-voucher. Coupon is delivered by email.
Coupon offers admission for up to 4 people (adults or children) at half price per person. All visitors after their first birthday require a ticket.
After receiving a library discount coupon, the Boston Children’s Museum recommends reserving your entry ticket with them online as museum capacity is limited at this time.
A library pass is not an admission ticket and does not guarantee a reservation to the Museum.
Reserve a Pass Reserve a Pass

Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there."} Boston Harbor Island Ferry 15 State Street, Suite 1100, Boston, MA 02109617-223-8666http://www.bostonharborislands.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston Harbor Island Ferry The largest recreational open space in Eastern Massachusetts, the Boston Harbor Islands National and State Park is comprised of 34 islands and mainland parks that deliver access to historic sites and provide opportunities to hike and camp. Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there.
Categories: Family, Nature, Recreation
Pass Benefits This is an e-voucher. Discount code is e-mailed to the patron.
The discount code provides access to purchase tickets at 50% off the regular price for the following:
Spectacle IslandMay 16 - June 15: Thursdays through SundaysJune 16 - Sep 1: every daySep 4 - 21: Thursdays through SundaysSep 26 - Oct 13: Fridays through Sundays
Georges IslandJune 16 - Sep 1: every day
Spectacle IslandJune 16 - Sep 1: every daySep 4 - Oct 13: Thursdays through Sundays
Ferries to Georges and Spectacle Islands are boarded at Long Wharf North in Downtown Boston. Ferries to Peddocks Island are boarded from Hingham.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "145 Redstone Hill, Sterling, MA 01564","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-davis-farmland_47_md.jpg","telephone": "978-422-6666","name": "Davis Farmland","url": "","description": "Davis Farmland is a seven-generation, working family farm, continuously in operation since 1846 in Sterling, MA. Starting in 1996, Davis Farmland has provided opportunities for discovery and play to families with children 12 years old and younger. It has a Waterspray Park; Imagine-Acres; Adventureland; interactions with farm animals; and a &ldquo;Mega Maze&rdquo; in the fall. It is also home to the largest sanctuary of endangered farm animals in North America. Special events include hands-on reptile shows. Hours change with the season. Shop with food and souvenirs on the premises."} Davis Farmland 145 Redstone Hill, Sterling, MA 01564978-422-6666https://www.davisfarmland.com/[Details/Reserve]
Davis Farmland Davis Farmland is a seven-generation, working family farm, continuously in operation since 1846 in Sterling, MA. Starting in 1996, Davis Farmland has provided opportunities for discovery and play to families with children 12 years old and younger. It has a Waterspray Park; Imagine-Acres; Adventureland; interactions with farm animals; and a “Mega Maze” in the fall. It is also home to the largest sanctuary of endangered farm animals in North America. Special events include hands-on reptile shows. Hours change with the season. Shop with food and souvenirs on the premises.
Categories: Children, Family, Nature, Zoo
Pass Type: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
Pass Benefits This is a paper coupon.
Patrons must pick up their pass at the Library up to one week before their visit. It does not need to be returned to the library.
Each pass admits up to 6 people to either farm park with a savings of $10 per person off the purchased-at-the-gate general admission rate. Children under 2 are always free.
Davis Farmland Discovery Farm is open mid-April - October.
Davis Mega Maze is open mid-September - October.
Pass is not valid with other discounts, groups or special events.
